regime_justification: USDJPY at 161.70, near the April intervention line (160) with BOJ having hiked to 1% on June 16, yet carry spread remains wide at 187 bps; vol subdued but mixed signals across JGB yields (2.63%) at multi-decade highs and crude oil falling sharply; intervention risk elevated but no imminent trigger today.

domino_stage.label: Pressure Building
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY 161.70, BOJ hiked to 1.0% on June 16 but spread still 187 bps, JGB 10Y at 2.63% (highest since 1995), Deputy Gov Himino reaffirmed tightening bias June 19, MOF intervention in April/May, oil collapsing post-Iran deal but import-cost pressure still present; carry mechanics intact yet policy tightening underway.

1w.label: Unlikely
1w.driver: BOJ just hiked; USDJPY consolidating post-hike; VIX 17.28, MOVE 70, oil down 21% in month; no imminent catalyst

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: Next BOJ MPM July 30–31; spread 187 bps still wide; JGB yields 2.63%, intervention risk remains if USDJPY tests 162-163; applied unwind prior; would otherwise grade D

3w.label: Watchful
3w.driver: BOJ meeting July 30-31 inside window; Himino signaled continued hikes if inflation exceeds 2%; Fed hawkish (Warsh tightening tone June 17); scenario-weighted for policy surprise

4w.label: Unlikely
4w.driver: Post-BOJ clarity likely reduces near-term tail risk; if no surprise hike, market reprices carry as durable into August; geopolitical de-escalation supportive

bottom_line: Carry trade still structurally intact (187 bps spread) despite BOJ's June hike to 1.0%; USDJPY at 161.70 is near intervention watch-line but consolidating; JGB yields at 2.63% and Deputy Gov Himino's June 19 commitment to further hikes mean slow-motion normalization continues. Oil down 21% in month removes near-term imported inflation pressure. Next BOJ meeting July 30–31 is the key catalyst. Grade 2-3 weeks at C (watchful) for potential hawkish surprise or USDJPY push above 162; otherwise regime remains yellow with pressure building but no imminent unwind. BTC at ~$63k reflects risk-off tilt but not panic.

trigger_map.trigger: Energy / Hormuz shock pushing ¥/bbl > prior peak
trigger_map.note: Oil down 21% in month; US-Iran 60-day deal, Hormuz reopening; ¥/bbl ~11,940, well below prior peak; tail risk receding

narrative.p1: Quiet consolidation after last week's BOJ hike. USDJPY at 161.70, up 6 bps on the day, holding just below 162 after the June 16 BOJ hike to 1.0%. Nikkei fell 3.6% from record highs on profit-taking and US tech weakness. Oil continued its collapse (down 2.3% today, -21% in month) on US-Iran 60-day roadmap and Hormuz reopening, removing near-term imported inflation pressure. US 10Y yield climbed 6 bps to 4.50% as markets digest hawkish Fed tone (Warsh) and anticipate Thursday PCE print; JGB 10Y at 2.63% (down 3 bps). No regime change from yesterday's yellow flag; carry spread remains 187 bps despite BOJ tightening. Deputy Governor Himino reaffirmed June 19 the BOJ will continue raising rates if inflation exceeds 2%, keeping July 30–31 MPM on watch.
narrative.p2: Carry mechanics: 187 bps spread (4.50% US vs 2.63% JGB) still incentivizes the trade despite BOJ hiking. USDJPY 1M implied vol unavailable but subdued given orderly price action. USDJPY 5-day change flat; distance from 60d mean is neutral/slightly stretched. Pace is not alarming—MOF historically reacts to rapid moves, not slow grinds. JGB yields at multi-decade highs signal normalization underway but not panic; MOVE at 70 (up from 65) shows rates vol rising but contained. Domino stage 1 (Pressure Building): BOJ rhetoric shifting (Himino June 19), JGB yields drifting up, spread still wide, USDJPY pace measured. Not yet at stage 2 (Pre-Intervention) because no rate-check rumors and USDJPY not at watch-line urgency. Grade 1w at D (unlikely): no imminent catalyst, just hiked, oil down. Grade 2w at C (watchful): applied unwind prior; would otherwise grade D. Next BOJ meeting July 30–31 sits inside 2-3 week window, and Himino signaled continued hikes; if USDJPY tests 162-163 or JGB yields spike further, intervention/hike risk elevates. Data is mixed (carry intact, vol low) but not clearly benign given JGB at 2.63% and BOJ tightening bias—tiebreaker pushes 2w to C.
narrative.p3: BTC at $63,010 (down 1.9% today, -3.4% in 7d) reflects the broader tech selloff and risk-off tilt but not systemic stress. Holding above $62k support; no waterfall. Crypto is tracking equity vol more than FX carry right now—Nikkei down 3.6%, US tech weak. XRP data unavailable. BTC open interest direction and DXY correlation not retrieved but BTC's decline is modest, suggesting crypto liquidity hasn't drained despite hawkish Fed and BOJ normalization. For positioning: BTC is a coincident indicator of risk appetite, not leading. If USDJPY breaks higher or BOJ surprises hawkish at July MPM, expect crypto to underperform further as liquidity tightens. Current setup: watch BTC $62k support and correlation with Nikkei; if both hold, crypto positioning can remain neutral. If BTC breaks $60k, risk-off intensifies.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: Week 1 graded D (unlikely) because BOJ just hiked June 16, USDJPY consolidating, oil down, no catalyst. Week 2 graded C (watchful) because next BOJ MPM July 30–31 is inside the 2-week forward window, Himino June 19 signaled more hikes if inflation hot, and USDJPY near 162 could draw MOF scrutiny. Applied unwind prior as tiebreaker; data mixed (carry wide but JGB yields high, vol low but normalization underway). Without prior, would grade D.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: Week 2 and 3 both graded C (watchful). BOJ meeting July 30–31 is the key event inside this window. If BOJ hikes another 25 bps or signals accelerated normalization, USDJPY could spike or compress depending on market positioning. Himino's June 19 comments ('continue to raise rates') and JGB yields at 2.63% mean another hike is plausible. Fed also hawkish (markets pricing 50% Sep hike). Scenario-weighted: 60% chance BOJ holds, 40% chance hike/hawkish signal. If hike, unwind risk rises to B; if hold, drops to D. Grade C reflects this uncertainty.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: Week 3 graded C, week 4 graded D (unlikely). By week 4, BOJ July MPM will have concluded and delivered clarity. If no surprise hike, market reprices carry as durable into August, unwinding pressure eases. If BOJ does hike, the immediate volatility spike will have occurred in week 3, and week 4 represents stabilization post-event. Geopolitical de-escalation (US-Iran deal) also supports lower tail risk by week 4. Grade D reflects post-catalyst normalization.

watch_list: BOJ MPM July 30-31: Himino signaled more hikes; watch for 25bp hike or hawkish forward guidance
USDJPY 162-163 test: Would trigger MOF rate-check rumors and intervention risk
US PCE data Thursday June 26: Fed's preferred inflation gauge; hawkish print could push US 10Y higher, widening spread further
JGB 10Y above 2.70%: Would mark new multi-decade high, potential bond volatility cascade
BTC break below $60k: Signal of broader crypto liquidity drain and risk-off intensification
